summary: Make the Gray-streams FORMAT redefinition unwrap a two-way stream to its output substream, like every other output function, plus regression tests.

items.title: FORMAT unwraps two-way streams to output substream + regression tests
items.description:
ROOT CAUSE: lib/gray-streams.lisp redefines CL:FORMAT (around lines 548-559, inside the big `let` that saves the original builtins). Every other redefined output function (write-char, write-string, princ, prin1, print, write, pprint) resolves its stream via the helper %resolve-output-stream (lines 296-300), which contains `(if (typep s 'two-way-stream) (two-way-stream-output-stream s) s)`. FORMAT does NOT call that helper: in its `(t ...)` branch it computes `(let ((s (if (eq destination t) *standard-output* destination))) ...)` and never unwraps a two-way stream. So when destination is a two-way stream wrapping Gray substreams (e.g. *terminal-io* / *debug-io* / *query-io*), `s` stays the two-way stream object; `(%gray-stream-p s)` is NIL (a two-way stream is a native CL_Stream, not a CLOS instance); FORMAT falls to `(apply orig-format destination ...)`; the C builtin's cl_stream_write_char -> resolve_stream(stream,1) selects the Gray output substream, but that substream is a CLOS instance for which CL_STREAM_P is false, so resolve_stream returns NIL and nothing is written. Result: FORMAT returns normally but writes nothing. Confirmed by reproduction: write-char to a Gray two-way stream writes correctly, but `(format tw "...")` produces no output.

FIX (lib/gray-streams.lisp, the FORMAT defun in the `(t ...)` branch): after mapping T to *standard-output*, unwrap a two-way stream to its output substream before the %gray-stream-p test. Replace
    (let ((s (if (eq destination t) *standard-output* destination)))
with
    (let* ((dest0 (if (eq destination t) *standard-output* destination))
           (s (if (typep dest0 'two-way-stream)
                  (two-way-stream-output-stream dest0)
                  dest0)))
Keep the rest of the body unchanged: if `s` is a Gray stream, format to a temp string-output-stream and write-string the result to `s` (returns NIL); otherwise `(apply orig-format destination control-string format-args)`. IMPORTANT: keep the NIL-destination fast path untouched (it must still return a string), and keep T mapping to *standard-output* (per HyperSpec, NOT *terminal-io* — this is why FORMAT must not simply reuse %resolve-output-stream, whose T maps to *terminal-io*). The else branch must still pass the ORIGINAL `destination` to orig-format so native (non-Gray) two-way streams keep working via the C-level unwrap (verified: native two-way + format still writes correctly).

REGRESSION TESTS — add a 'FORMAT to a Gray two-way stream' check to both existing Gray two-way test blocks, mirroring the existing write-char/write-string checks:
  (a) trunk/test-gray-streams.lisp: in the both-Gray two-way `let*` block (around lines 154-169, binds gin/gcap/tw and currently checks `gray tw: write accumulates`), add e.g. `(format tw "f~D" 9)` then `(check "gray tw: format delegates" "f9" (gray-cap-flush gcap))`. Use a fresh write/flush so it doesn't collide with the existing `write accumulates` assertion (either flush before, or assert the combined accumulated string).
  (b) tests/amiga/run-tests.lisp: in the both-Gray two-way block (around lines 4729-4744, binds gin/gcap/tw, helper gs-flush, ends with `gray tw write accumulates`), add an analogous `(format tw "f~D" 9)` + `(check "gray tw format delegates" "f9" (gs-flush gcap))`. Read only the relevant region with offset/limit — do NOT read the whole 4778-line file.

VALIDATION: `make host` then a scratch script that (load "lib/gray-streams.lisp"), defines a capturing fundamental-character-output-stream and an input Gray stream, wraps them with make-two-way-stream, and asserts `(format tw "hello~D" 42)` makes the captured buffer equal "hello42". Also run `./build/host/clamiga --heap 24M --load trunk/test-gray-streams.lisp` and confirm ALL TESTS PASSED. Run `make test` and confirm it stays green.
